# Contributing to Grafyx

Thank you for your interest in contributing to Grafyx! We welcome contributions from the community to help make this high-performance code knowledge graph tool better.

## How to Contribute

### Reporting Bugs
1. Check the [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/0xarchit/grafyx/issues) to see if the bug has already been reported.
2. If not, open a new issue using the **Bug Report** template.
3. Provide as much detail as possible, including steps to reproduce, expected behavior, and screenshots if applicable.

### Suggesting Features
1. Check the [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/0xarchit/grafyx/issues) for existing feature requests.
2. If your idea is new, open an issue using the **Feature Request** template.
3. Describe the feature clearly and explain why it would be beneficial to the project.

### Submitting Code
1. Fork the repository.
2. Create a new branch for your feature or bugfix (`git checkout -b feature/amazing-feature`).
3. Commit your changes with descriptive commit messages.
4. Push your branch to your fork.
5. Open a Pull Request (PR) against the `main` branch of the Grafyx repository.
6. Ensure your PR description clearly explains the changes and links to any relevant issues.

## Code Style
Please adhere to the existing code style and conventions used in the project. If you are introducing new dependencies, please justify their need in the PR description.

## License
By contributing to Grafyx, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the project's [Apache License 2.0](../LICENSE).

## Questions?
If you have any questions, feel free to contact the maintainer at [mail@0xarchit.is-a.dev](mailto:mail@0xarchit.is-a.dev).
